summary: Round 2 backend verification (safety filter + regression). All P0 SAFETY_BLOCKED cases pass across all 3 AI endpoints (/api/ai/suggestions, /api/ai/first-message, /api/ai/screenshot) — each correctly returns HTTP 422 with body { detail: { code: 'SAFETY_BLOCKED', message: <string> } } when given explicit/nudity context. Regression suite for benign AI inputs, auth (dev-login, /auth/me, logout), subscription (status, start-trial, upgrade simulated), history CRUD, and settings all green. Total: 20 passed, 1 skipped (exclusive-no-sub test requires direct Mongo access — same skip as iteration 1, not a regression).
critical_code_review_comments: build_system_message: safety instructions are concatenated unconditionally for all modes (normal/flirty/exclusive) — correct.
check_safety_block scans every parsed suggestion for the SAFETY_BLOCK_TOKEN sentinel; works even when Claude wraps the sentinel array in markdown code-fences (parse_json_list strips them).
All 3 AI endpoints raise HTTPException(422, detail={code, message}) BEFORE returning suggestions, and re-raise inside the broad try/except — so the 422 is not swallowed into 500.

FastAPI auto-wraps the dict detail under top-level 'detail' key — verified empirically (body shape: { detail: { code: 'SAFETY_BLOCKED', message: '...' } }) — matches frontend SafetyNotice expectations.
Screenshot endpoint: explicit extra_context alone (with benign image) is sufficient to trigger SAFETY_BLOCKED — confirms Claude reads the extra_context block in addition to the image.
No deprecated expo-av / expo-barcode-scanner / expo-background-fetch usage observed in backend.
server.py still under 600 lines, well within the 700-line guideline.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_loveassist_backend.py (replaced TestSafetyFilter with 4 new tests covering 422 SAFETY_BLOCKED across /ai/suggestions, /ai/first-message, /ai/screenshot, plus a benign regression case)
seed_data_creation: Per-test isolated demo users via /api/auth/dev-login using TEST_-prefixed emails; ephemeral history items created and deleted within the same test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Round 2 SAFETY_BLOCKED behavior is implemented via a sentinel token '__SAFETY_BLOCK__' embedded in Claude's JSON response, then detected by check_safety_block() in server.py. The error body shape is FastAPI-standard: { detail: { code: 'SAFETY_BLOCKED', message: '...' } } — frontend code reading err.response.data.detail.code is correct. To trigger reliably, include explicit terms like 'nude', 'explicit', 'graphic intercourse' in context/about_person/extra_context. Claude obeys the system prompt even in flirty/exclusive modes. The screenshot endpoint triggers SAFETY_BLOCKED based on extra_context alone when the image is benign.
rca_of_issue: N/A — all Round 2 changes verified working.
